name: moa-bridge
displayName: MoA Bridge - Mixture of Agents for Copilot
description: Mixture-of-Agents for VSCode Copilot Chat. Runs multi-LLM fan-out (recon → N refs → aggregator → acting agent) through the native vscode.lm API. Supports @moa chat participant, moa_recon / moa_analyze / moa_orchestrate LM tools, preset groups, and parallel reference advisors. Vendor-agnostic — works with Copilot, GCMP, or any extension exposing LanguageModelChat.

chatParticipants.name: moa
chatParticipants.description: v0.17.0: MoA Loop mode (default) — iterative 5-role pipeline until Aggregator naturally converges

disambiguation.description: Multi-perspective analysis with multiple LLM advisors (iterative loop mode)

chatParticipants.name: moaloop
chatParticipants.description: v0.17.0: Explicit Loop mode — same as @moa (iterative until Aggregator decides finalize or MAX_ITER)

disambiguation.description: Explicit MoA Loop mode (iterative refinement until natural convergence)

chatParticipants.name: moasingle
chatParticipants.description: v0.17.0: Single-shot mode — 1 iteration then forces finalize (faster but may have low confidence if evidence is incomplete)

disambiguation.description: MoA Single-shot mode (fast, 1 iteration only; use @moa or @moaloop for complex tasks)

commands.title: MoA: Configure Models
commands.description: v0.14.14: Five-step flow — (0/4) pick/create/delete preset group, (1/4) refs (checkbox multi-select, 2-8), (2/4) aggregator (single-select checkbox), (3/4) recon agent (single-select, default=aggregator), (4/4) L3 summarizer (single-select + 'Disable L3' option). All steps use checkbox UI for clarity of current selection.

commands.title: MoA: Switch Preset
commands.description: v0.14.14: QuickPick to switch the active preset group. Shows all saved presets with a preview of which models they use (refs count + aggregator + recon + L3). One-click switch, no need to re-configure models.

commands.title: MoA: Probe Available Tools (Debug)
commands.description: Lists all tools currently registered in vscode.lm.tools. Use this to check if Copilot/other extensions expose tools that MoA's acting agent could call.

languageModelTools.name: moa_recon

prompt.description: The recon task description. e.g. 'gather all code related to MoA recon pipeline'.

scopeHint.description: Optional: hint about what files/symbols to look at. Supports paths with line ranges, e.g. 'src/moaRunner.ts:120-200 src/types.ts'. The tool extracts path:line-line patterns and reads those exact ranges.

includeWorkspaceTree.description: Include the project tree in the result (default true). Useful for giving downstream tools a sense of structure.

maxFiles.description: Maximum number of files to read (default 8, max 20).

languageModelTools.modelDescription: Read-only recon subagent that gathers file contents relevant to a task and returns them as Markdown. Good for inspecting multiple files at once when you need broader context before deeper analysis. USE THIS before calling #moaAnalyze or #moaOrchestrate when the user's question references specific files or code — pass this tool's output as reconContext to ground the MoA analysis. If you only need a single file, prefer copilot_readFile directly.
languageModelTools.userDescription:
MoA recon subagent — read-only file collection. Gathers relevant file contents for the main LLM to inspect or pass forward to moa_analyze.

Workflow (Hermes-style):
1. When the user's question references specific files or code, call moa_recon FIRST with a scopeHint.
2. moa_recon returns file contents as a structured Markdown tool result.
3. Then call moa_analyze with reconContext=<the gathered content from step 2>.

If the user asks a general question without file references, skip moa_recon and call moa_analyze directly — MoA's internal Phase 0 recon will gather files itself.

languageModelTools.name: moa_analyze

prompt.description: The question or task to analyze. Pass the user's original question verbatim.

forceDirect.description: Optional: skip the multi-model consultation and use a single acting agent. Useful when MoA setup is broken or fast single-model analysis is preferred. Default false.

maxReconRounds.description: Optional: override the number of recon rounds (default 3). Lower = faster but less grounded; higher = more thorough but slower/costlier.

collectRawFiles.description: Optional: include a hint in the result to suggest reading source files directly. Default false.

reconContext.description: v0.10.2: Pre-collected context (file contents, grep results, etc.) that the calling LLM gathered via its own tool calls (e.g. copilot_readFile). When provided, MoA skips its internal Phase 0 recon and uses this directly as the ref prompt's RECON DATA block. Recommended: call copilot_readFile first to read the relevant file(s), then pass their concatenated contents here. This implements the Hermes-style subagent flow where recon is a separate tool the orchestrator decides to call.

reconSources.description: v0.10.2: List of file paths that produced the reconContext (for logging/traceability). Example: ['src/moaRunner.ts', 'src/types.ts'].

languageModelTools.modelDescription: v0.17.0: Run SINGLE-SHOT (1 iteration only) 5-role MoA analysis (Planner → Recon → Refs → Aggregator → Actor). Forces finalize after iteration 1 regardless of Aggregator's actual suggestion — completeness may be < 80% if evidence is incomplete. For iterative refinement (let Aggregator decide convergence), USE #moaOrchestrate instead. Good for: single-shot questions needing multi-model reasoning (code review, design trade-offs). NOT for: complex tasks requiring iterative recon refinement. Optional reconContext parameter accepts pre-gathered file contents (e.g. from #moaRecon or copilot_readFile) so refs give grounded analysis.
languageModelTools.userDescription:
v0.17.0: Run the MoA 5-role pipeline in SINGLE-SHOT mode (Planner → Recon → Refs → Aggregator → Actor, 1 iteration only). Output is persisted to .moa_cache/<task_id>/ (final.md + timeline.md).

**When to use this vs #moa_orchestrate**:
- `#moa_analyze` — single-shot (fast, but Aggregator may want more iterations)
- `#moa_orchestrate` — iterative loop (Aggregator decides convergence, more thorough)

**Recommended workflow (Hermes-style subagent)**:
1. Call #moaRecon first to gather relevant file contents
2. Pass that content as reconContext=<markdown content> to #moaAnalyze
3. MoA refs see the pre-collected context and produce grounded analysis

For simple questions, answer directly without MoA.

**Intermediate output**: VSCode Output panel (View → Output) shows per-role channels: MoA Planner / MoA Recon / MoA Refs / MoA Aggregator / MoA Actor.

languageModelTools.name: moa_orchestrate

task.description: The task to orchestrate through the iterative MoA loop. Should be the user's full original request, including any relevant context.

languageModelTools.modelDescription: Launch a multi-round Mixture-of-Agents orchestration loop for complex tasks that need iterative refinement. Good for: research with multiple unknowns, multi-file refactoring plans, design decisions with trade-offs, anything where a single MoA pass won't be enough. Each round runs N worker LLMs in parallel + an aggregator that fuses outputs and identifies gaps. When gaps need external info, you (the main agent) call #runSubagent to fill them and feed results back via #moaContinue. Loop stops at completeness ≥ 0.8 or 12 iterations, then you call #moaFinalize to get action items. USE THIS instead of #moaAnalyze when the task is hard enough to need 3+ rounds of refinement. For simple single-shot analysis, prefer #moaAnalyze.
languageModelTools.userDescription:
Start a new iterative MoA orchestration loop (v0.12.0). The orchestrator runs worker LLMs + aggregator in a multi-round loop, persisting state to .moa_cache/<task_id>/. Each iteration may produce gaps that need recon; when that happens, the result tells you to call #runSubagent and then #moa_continue.

Use this for complex tasks that require iterative refinement: research, multi-file refactoring plans, design decisions with trade-offs. Unlike moa_analyze (single-shot), this tool loops until completeness ≥ 0.8 or max 12 iterations.

Workflow:
1. Call #moa_orchestrate(task) → get task_id + initial status
2. Loop: if status='awaiting_recon', call #runSubagent with the provided prompt, then #moa_continue(task_id, subagent_result=<runSubagent output>); if status='running', just call #moa_continue(task_id)
3. When status='finalized' or you want to stop, call #moa_finalize(task_id) → get action_items
4. Execute action_items using your own tools (writeFile, execute, etc.)

Models used: moa.refModels (workers) + moa.aggregator (aggregator/finalizer). Configure via 'Moa: Configure Models' command.

languageModelTools.name: moa_continue

subagent_result.description: Optional: result from #runSubagent that fills the gaps identified in the previous iteration. Omit if no recon was needed (status='running').

languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Advance an MoA orchestration loop by one iteration (started via #moaOrchestrate). Use this to drive the loop forward. Two modes:
- If the previous status was 'awaiting_recon': you MUST first call #runSubagent with the gaps prompt provided in the previous result, then pass its output here as subagent_result.
- If the previous status was 'running': just call this with task_id only (no subagent_result) to advance another round.
Continue calling until status='finalized' or you decide to stop, then call #moaFinalize.
languageModelTools.userDescription:
Advance the MoA orchestration loop by one iteration (v0.12.0). Run this after #moa_orchestrate to advance. If the previous status was 'awaiting_recon', you MUST have called #runSubagent first and pass its output as subagent_result. Otherwise, omit subagent_result to advance without new recon.

The result includes the current synthesis, completeness, gaps, and next-step instruction.

languageModelTools.name: moa_finalize
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Force-finalize an MoA orchestration loop and produce concrete action items. Use this when the loop has converged (previous status was 'finalized') OR when you want to stop early and get the current best answer. Returns action_items to execute: write_file (with target=path and full content), execute (target=shell command), create_roadmap (target=plan title), research_more (target=follow-up topic), inform_user (target=subject + message). After receiving action_items, execute them using your own tools (copilot_createFile for files, run_in_terminal for commands, etc.).
languageModelTools.userDescription:
Force-finalize an MoA orchestration loop and produce action items (v0.12.0). Call this when the loop has converged (status='finalized') OR when you want to stop early. Returns a JSON-shaped Markdown with: summary, action_items[{type, target, content, rationale}], confidence, unresolved.

Action item types: write_file (target=path, content=full text), execute (target=command), create_roadmap (target=title), research_more (target=topic), inform_user (target=subject).

After receiving action_items, execute them using your own tools (writeFile, run_in_terminal, etc.).

moa.refModels.description: Reference advisor models (equal-mode MoA). Each item: {role, model}. All refs share the same prompt (moa.sharedRefPrompt); `role` is a display label only, NOT a role specialization. Use 'Moa: Configure Models' command to set this interactively.

role.description: Display label (e.g. 'advisor_1'). Not injected into prompt.

model.description: Substring to match vscode.lm model name, e.g. 'GLM-5.2'

systemHint.description: Deprecated in v2 (equal mode). Kept for backward compat, ignored at runtime.
systemHint.deprecationMessage: Equal-mode MoA ignores per-ref systemHint. Use moa.sharedRefPrompt instead.

moa.aggregator.description: Aggregator (synthesizer) model. Use 'Moa: Configure Models' command to set interactively.

model.description: Substring to match vscode.lm model name

temperature.description: Informational only (vscode.lm doesn't expose per-call temp yet).

moa.parallelRefs.description: v0.14.14: Fan out reference advisors in parallel (Promise.allSettled). When true (default), all ref requests fire simultaneously → wall-clock time = slowest ref. When false, refs run sequentially (safer for providers that rate-limit concurrent requests). NOTE: pre-v0.14.14 this setting existed but was never read by code; v0.14.14 makes it actually work.

moa.parallelRecon.description: v0.18.0: Fan out Recon agents in parallel when preset.reconModels has 2+ models. When true (default), all recon agents run simultaneously → wall-clock time = slowest. When false, or when only 1 recon model is configured, runs sequentially (single-model mode) and Recon Aggregator is auto-disabled. Parallel recon produces N independent results that are merged by Recon Aggregator (pure LLM integration, no tool calls) into a single summary before injecting to Refs.

moa.presets.description: v0.14.14: Named preset groups. Each value is a full pipeline config: {name, refModels, aggregator, reconModel, l3Summarizer, description?}. Switch active preset via 'Moa: Switch Preset' command. Legacy flat config (moa.refModels + moa.aggregator + ...) is auto-migrated to presets.default on first use.

moa.activePreset.description: v0.14.14: Key into moa.presets identifying the currently active group. Switch via 'Moa: Switch Preset' command. When the referenced preset doesn't exist, falls back to the first available preset, then to legacy flat config.

moa.sharedRefPrompt.description: Optional override for the shared prompt prepended to every ref query. If empty (recommended), uses the built-in Hermes _REFERENCE_SYSTEM_PROMPT ported from NousResearch/hermes-agent. All refs get the SAME prompt — diversity comes from model differences, not role assignment.

enumDescriptions: Hermes-style: refs shown only as progress indicators ('Ref advisor_1 thinking...'). Detailed ref output goes to the 'MoA Bridge — Ref Output' panel. Ref content does NOT enter chat history — only the aggregator synthesis does. Matches Hermes' design where refs are side-channel advisory calls.
Legacy: refs stream inline as markdown in the chat response. Visible AND recorded in chat history. Use this if you want Copilot follow-ups to reference individual ref opinions.
moa.refDisplayMode.description: How to display reference advisor output in the chat UI. 'thinking' (default) keeps refs out of chat history (Hermes-style); 'verbose' shows them inline.

moa.enableActingAgent.description: v0.8.0: Enable the 3-layer Hermes architecture. When true, the aggregator's output becomes guidance for an acting agent that can call tools (read_file, find_files, apply_patch, run_in_terminal, etc.) to actually accomplish the user's task. When false, falls back to v0.7.x 2-layer behavior (aggregator output IS the final answer). Requires copilot_* tools to be registered (default when GitHub Copilot Chat is active).

moa.enableRecon.description: v0.9.0: Enable the recon phase (Phase 0). When true, a recon agent runs BEFORE ref fan-out to collect files relevant to the user's question (read_file, find_files, grep, get_errors, search_codebase). The collected raw context is injected into ref prompts so refs can give grounded analysis instead of guessing. When false, skips recon — refs see only workspace context (v0.8.0 behavior).

moa.maxReconRounds.description: v0.9.0: Maximum number of recon rounds. Each round = 1 recon pass + N ref fan-out. If refs report 'sufficient=false' in their JSON output, the next recon round uses their 'missing' hints to fill gaps. Loop exits early when a majority of refs say sufficient=true. Hard cap at 5 to bound cost.

moa.reconContextChars.description: v0.14.5 [DEPRECATED — 不再作上限]: 仅作为审计指标记录到 meta.json。原 v0.13.0 用于总字符预算截断，v0.14.5 起完全不再截断 —— refs 单轮无历史 + 1M 上下文模型可以消化任意大小；如果 recon 真爆了说明检索方向出问题（噪声过多），是 LLM 该处理的不是工程层该截断的。保留此配置仅为向后兼容。

moa.forceDirect.description: v0.9.1: Skip the entire recon/ref/aggregator pipeline. The acting agent receives only the user prompt + workspace context and acts directly (no multi-model consultation). Useful when the user has had repeated multi-model failures and just wants a single competent model to handle the task with full tool access.

moa.maxReconIterations.description: v0.13.0: Recon agent 单次任务的最大工具调用迭代数（兜底硬上限）。默认 50。实际多数任务会在 LLM 自主判断 + 早停机制触发后更早收敛。配置项 moa.reconEarlyStopStagnant / moa.reconEarlyStopSaturated 控制主动早停。

moa.reconAllowTerminal.description: v0.13.0: 允许 recon agent 使用 terminal 类工具（runInTerminal / sendToTerminal / killTerminal）。默认 false（安全为先）。Debug 工作流需要 recon 跑测试/编译时设为 true。注意：prompt 会指示 recon 避免破坏性命令（rm -rf / git push --force 等），但最终取决于模型遵循度。

moa.reconEarlyStopStagnant.description: v0.13.0: Recon 早停阈值 —— 连续 N 轮工具签名（工具名+输入摘要）完全相同 → 触发早停（防死循环）。默认 2。设大可让 recon 更'耐心'，设小可更激进省成本。

moa.reconEarlyStopSaturated.description: v0.13.0: Recon 早停阈值 —— iterations > 5 后，单轮新增 capturedToolCalls 字符数 < 此值持续 N 轮（N 同 reconEarlyStopStagnant）→ 触发早停（信息饱和）。默认 200 字符。

moa.reconL3Threshold.description: v0.14.4: L3 孙代理触发阈值。单个文件超过此字符数且为代码文件时，才派 L3 做内容精选（保留关键函数）。v0.14.4 默认从 60k 提升到 200k —— 现代 LLM 1M 上下文，绝大多数文件无需精选。只有真正巨型文件（如生成的 schema、minified bundle）才需要 L3。

moa.reconL3MaxCalls.description: v0.13.0: 单次 MoA 任务最多派多少个 L3 孙代理。超出后即使文件超大也降级为 L2 语义边界截断。设 0 完全禁用 L3。默认 5（兼顾效果与成本）。

moa.reconL3TargetChars.description: v0.14.4: L3 孙代理对单个大文件精选后的目标输出长度（字符）。默认 50k —— v0.14.3 是 10k，过度压缩。1M 上下文模型完全可以消化 50k 的精选结果。

moa.reconModel.description: v0.14.0: Recon agent 专用模型配置。model 字段为 vscode.lm 模型 ID（如 gcmp.zhipu:::GLM-5.2-CodingPlan）。空字符串 = fallback 到 aggregator 模型（保持 v0.13.x 行为）。通过 'Moa: Configure Models' 命令的 Step 3/4 设置。

model.description: Recon 模型 ID。空 = 复用 aggregator（向后兼容）。

temperature.description: 保留字段（vscode.lm API 暂未使用）。

moa.l3Summarizer.description: v0.14.0: L3 孙代理（大文件精选器）模型配置。model 字段为 vscode.lm 模型 ID。空字符串 = 禁用 L3（所有大文件降级为 L2 语义边界截断）。推荐 MiniMax-M3 (TokenPlan)。通过 'Moa: Configure Models' 命令的 Step 4/4 设置。

model.description: L3 模型 ID。空 = 禁用 L3。
